title: OSCORE seqnum increment wraps in release, violating security invariant
description:
# Problem
In `rust/lichen-oscore/src/seqnum.rs:52-55`:

```rust
pub fn increment(self) -> Self {
    debug_assert!(self.0 < u32::MAX, "OSCORE sequence number overflow - context must be renegotiated");
    Self(self.0.wrapping_add(1))
}
```

The assertion only fires in debug builds. In release, wrapping proceeds silently.

## What goes wrong
OSCORE sequence number wraparound causes nonce reuse, which is a critical security violation (RFC 8613 Section 7.2.1). An attacker who observes two messages encrypted with the same nonce can XOR the ciphertexts to recover plaintext.

While `protect_request` in `lib.rs` does check for `u32::MAX` before calling `fetch_increment`, a direct caller of `OscoreSeqNum::increment()` will get silent wraparound.

## Trigger
- Call `OscoreSeqNum::new(u32::MAX).increment()` in release mode
- Result: Returns `OscoreSeqNum(0)` instead of erroring

## Fix
Option 1: Return `Option<Self>` from `increment()` to force callers to handle exhaustion
Option 2: Mark `increment` unsafe/deprecated and direct callers to `protect_request` only
Option 3: Change to `checked_add()` and panic on overflow even in release (fail-safe > fail-secure for crypto)
close_reason: Fixed in worktrees, verified by codereview passes
